todaysPlan.activity: Mixed-vowel CVC phoneme segmentation diagnostic (sit, sat, hit, hat, bit)
todaysPlan.reason: 19 days overdue since 3/14. Critical gate-keeper assessment determining true phonemic awareness vs word-family pattern recognition. All decoding instruction depends on this baseline. Zero mixed-vowel data exists despite 100% accuracy on isolated /a/ and /i/ words. Must execute before any phoneme advancement.
todaysPlan.method: Sound boxes with three highlighted boxes per word. Present words in alternating /i/→/a/→/i/→/a/→/i/ sequence. Single-step directions only: 'Tell me all three sounds in [word]. Ready? Go!' Record exact phoneme production for each position (initial/medial/final). Success = 80%+ accuracy (4/5 words).
todaysPlan.source: CELF-5 profile (Sentence Comprehension 63rd percentile = use narrative framing; Word Classes 100% with visuals = provide sound boxes; Following Directions 2nd percentile = single-step only). Wilson methodology requires segmentation before decoding.
probeSequence: Repeat for hit, hat, bit in rapid sequence

todaysPlan.reason: CELF-5 2nd percentile. IEP-mandated baseline. 29 planning mentions but zero documented execution across 20+ sessions. Legal compliance requirement—progress monitoring cannot occur without actual performance data.
todaysPlan.method: Present two-step directions with modifiers in natural session context. (1) 'Touch the big red circle on the screen, then clap your hands twice.' (2) 'Pick up the blue marker, then put it next to the book.' Record: completed fully / completed partially (which step) / did not complete. Use 'wait until I say Go' prompt before each direction.
todaysPlan.source: CELF-5 Following Directions subtest protocol. Natalie's 4/1 Zoom noted 'needed repeated reminders to slow down'—consistent with documented deficit but no formal baseline exists.

todaysPlan.activity: Recalling Sentences formal auditory memory baseline probe
todaysPlan.reason: CELF-5 5th percentile. IEP-mandated baseline. 25 planning mentions but zero sentence repetition tasks across all sessions. Essential for progress monitoring; auditory memory breakdown impacts all language learning.
todaysPlan.method: Present sentences for immediate repetition. (1) 'The dog ran fast' (4 words). (2) 'The big cat is sleeping on the soft chair' (9 words, 2 modifiers). (3) 'When the boy came home from school, he found his red ball by the fence' (15 words, embedded clause). Record verbatim responses; note omissions, substitutions, word order errors.
todaysPlan.source: CELF-5 Recalling Sentences subtest protocol. Evaluator noted breakdown with increasing sentence length/complexity.

todaysPlan.activity: Word Structure oral morphology probe (irregular plurals, present progressive, past tense)
todaysPlan.reason: CELF-5 5th percentile. 21 planning mentions but only spelling tested (addition, forgotten, work/worked)—CELF-5 documents oral production errors. Must assess morphological accuracy under demand, not just orthographic recall.
todaysPlan.method: Elicit oral responses with visual prompts. (1) Show picture of one mouse, then two mice: 'Here is one mouse. Here are two ___.' (2) Show picture of cat eating: 'The cat is ___.' (3) Show picture of completed action + card 'jump': 'Yesterday the cat ___.' (4) Show picture of girl running: '___ is running.' Record exact verbal responses.
todaysPlan.source: CELF-5 Word Structure subtest protocol. Documented errors: irregular plurals (mice→mouses, children→childs), present progressive (-ing inconsistent), subjective pronouns (she/they).

todaysPlan.activity: Consonant doubling + complex suffix spelling (careful, fearful, helpless)
todaysPlan.reason: Ila mastered consonant doubling (hopped, sitting, running 86% accuracy) and base+suffix transformations (move/movers/moving, work/worked). Ready for -ful/-less suffix patterns—these are high-frequency and morphologically productive.
todaysPlan.method: Word Builder rounds 1-4 for each word, then spell from memory. Use typing if voice spelling shows errors (as with 'moving' on 4/1). Break into base + suffix explicitly: 'care + ful = careful.' Provide visual model after first attempt if needed.
skipConditions: If mixed-vowel probe shows <60% accuracy and requires extended re-teaching
If Following Directions or Recalling Sentences probes reveal severe deficits requiring immediate intervention planning
If Ila shows fatigue after required probes (has occurred before at 10-15 minute mark)

todaysPlan.reason: CELF-5 16th percentile. 8 planning mentions but never formally probed. All sentences in sessions are self-generated narratives ('A man tucked a wig...')—CELF-5 documents dropped stimulus words and subject-verb agreement errors under demand.
todaysPlan.method: Provide constraints and elicit sentence generation. (1) 'Make a sentence with the word cat.' (2) Show action picture: 'Make a sentence about what you see.' (3) 'Make a sentence using the words dog and run.' Record exact sentences; note grammatical errors, dropped words, incomplete formulations.
todaysPlan.source: CELF-5 Formulated Sentences subtest protocol. Evaluator noted errors like 'my cat and my dog is playing' (subject-verb agreement).
skipConditions: If required probes consume >20 minutes (attention limit)
If Ila requests break or shows shutdown behaviors
If session must end due to external time constraint (school, meal)

childProfile: Ila is an 8-year-old with ADHD (combined type) and dyslexia. Her brain works best with visuals—she scored 100% on language tasks when pictures were present but only 37.5% without them. She understands sentences well (63rd percentile) but struggles to follow multi-step directions (2nd percentile) and remember sentences she hears (5th percentile). Her working memory is low, so instructions must be broken into single steps with a clear 'Ready? Go!' signal. She rushes when anxious and shuts down if she feels wrong, so praise effort over accuracy. She's friendly, willing, and lights up with encouragement. Use sound boxes, visual anchors, and narrative framing ('We're playing vowel detective!'). Keep sessions under 15 minutes before fatigue hits. Never compare her to her twin Reina—they learn differently.
stopAfter: After 25-30 minutes total, or when Ila shows fatigue signs (requests to stop, multiple false starts, off-topic questions), or after completing the required probes (mixed-vowel segmentation, Following Directions, Recalling Sentences, Word Structure oral) if attention remains high.
rewardPolicy: Ila is motivated by structured game time (Word Builder, Space Invaders, magic tricks) but negotiates boundaries. Offer brief game breaks (2-3 minutes) after completing 2-3 required probes, not before word work. If she asks for games before tasks, redirect gently: 'First we do [task], then we play.' Use games as rewards for effort, not just accuracy—she needs to feel successful even when learning is hard. Avoid extended gaming (>5 minutes) mid-session; save longer play for end-of-session reward if all required work is complete.
